_comment: California Public Employees' Retirement System (CalPERS) — Defined Benefit pension data. Consumer-agnostic data only. No Meridian-specific references. Representative formula set: Classic Misc 2%@55, Classic Misc 2%@60, PEPRA Misc 2%@62, Classic Safety 3%@50, PEPRA Safety 2.7%@57. CalPERS has 3,000+ contracting employers; this file models the most prevalent formulas across member categories.
administeredBy: CalPERS — governed by the Board of Administration (13 members). Largest defined-benefit public pension in the United States.

totalMembers.note: 2M+ members in CalPERS retirement system as of 2025. Additionally administers health benefits for 1.5M+ members and families.

populations: California state employees (executive, legislative, judicial branches)
California school classified staff and non-instructional employees
Employees of ~3,000 contracting public agencies (cities, counties, special districts)
Safety members: law enforcement officers, firefighters, and other designated safety positions
excluded: California public school teachers preK-12 (covered by CalSTRS)
University of California employees (covered by UCRP)
City of Los Angeles employees (covered by LACERS/LAFPP)
Some independent city/county pension systems (not contracted with CalPERS)

memberCategories.miscellaneous: All non-safety members — most state and local government employees
memberCategories.safety: Law enforcement, fire prevention, and other positions designated as safety by law or CalPERS determination

socialSecurity.note: SS coverage varies by employer and bargaining unit. Most state miscellaneous members participate in Social Security. Many local agency members do not. Safety members generally do not participate in SS. Non-SS members pay a contribution rate 1% higher than SS-covered members under the same formula.

pepra.description: Public Employees' Pension Reform Act (PEPRA) established new benefit formulas and pensionable compensation caps for 'new members' — employees first hired to a CalPERS-covered position on or after January 1, 2013, or those returning after a break in service of more than six months with a different employer.
pepra.membershipShare: ~68% of CalPERS active members are PEPRA members as of 2026; projected to reach 90% within a decade.
keyChanges: Lower benefit factors for miscellaneous and safety formulas
Higher normal retirement age (62 misc, 57 safety)
Final compensation based on 36-month average (vs. 1-year option for some Classic formulas)
Pensionable compensation cap limiting salary used in benefit calculation
Employee contributions set at 50% of total normal cost (fluctuates annually)

vesting.note: 5 years of service credit required for vested retirement benefit under all CalPERS formulas.

classic_irc_401a17.description: IRS section 401(a)(17) compensation limit for Classic members. Members with membership dates prior to July 1, 1996 are not subject to this limit.

pepra_with_ss_2026.description: PEPRA pensionable compensation cap for members who coordinate with Social Security — calendar year 2026.

pepra_without_ss_2026.description: PEPRA pensionable compensation cap for members who do not coordinate with Social Security — calendar year 2026.

cola.description: COLA is applied annually as a compounded percentage against the member's base allowance (gross benefit at retirement). The annual adjustment equals the lesser of the employer-contracted COLA percentage or the CPI rate of inflation since retirement.
cola.firstEligibility: Second calendar year of retirement. Example: retire in 2024, first COLA in May 2026.

contractedRates.employers: All state agencies and all school agencies; approximately 95% of all CalPERS retirees
contractedRates.note: 2% maximum compounded annual COLA — most common. If inflation is below 2%, the lower inflation rate applies.

contractedRates.employers: Select public agency employers by contract
Select public agency employers by contract
Select public agency employers by contract

cola.note: Public agencies can contract for 2%, 3%, 4%, or 5% COLA. Retirees with 4% or 5% COLA provisions generally will not qualify for PPPA since higher COLAs maintain purchasing power above minimum thresholds.

pppa.name: Purchasing Power Protection Allowance
pppa.description: Supplemental benefit paid annually in the May 1 warrant. Activates when a retiree's cumulative COLA has not kept pace with inflation and their purchasing power falls below the threshold. PPPA is the difference between the retiree's current allowance and the minimum threshold amount.
pppa.note: State and school retirees are protected at 75% of original purchasing power; public agency retirees at 80%. PPPA is only payable when gross benefit drops below threshold and is calculated after COLA. Retirees with 4% or 5% COLA contracts generally remain above PPPA thresholds.

representativeFormulas._note: CalPERS has 3,000+ contracting employers with varying benefit tiers. This section models the five most prevalent formula types: two Classic miscellaneous, one PEPRA miscellaneous, one Classic safety, and one PEPRA safety. Additional Classic safety formulas (2%@50, State Safety 2.5%@55, State Safety B 2%@55) exist and are summarized in additionalFormulas.

classic_misc_2pct_at_55.prevalence: Most common Classic miscellaneous formula for state employees and many local agency employees hired before January 1, 2013.

formula.structure: Age Factor × Years of Service Credit × Final Compensation = Annual Pension

ageFactor.note: Age factor increases by quarter-year increments. Maximum benefit factor is 2.418% at age 63 and older. Full quarter-year tables available from CalPERS.

state_members.description: Highest single year (12 consecutive months) of average monthly pay rate
state_members.note: Applies to state miscellaneous/industrial members. 1-year final compensation is a key Classic advantage vs PEPRA.

local_members.description: Average annual compensation over highest 36 consecutive months
local_members.note: Most local agency contracts use 36-month final compensation.

normalRetirement.note: Unreduced benefit available at age 55 with 5 years of service

earlyRetirement.note: Members may retire at age 50 with 5 years of service, subject to reduced age factor

state_ss_covered.note: 7% of monthly pensionable compensation for state employees participating in Social Security — set by statute

state_non_ss.note: 8% for state employees not participating in Social Security (1% higher than SS-covered rate)

local_varies.note: Local agency Classic member contribution rates vary by contract. Typical range 7-8% for miscellaneous members.

classic_misc_2pct_at_60.prevalence: Common second-tier Classic miscellaneous formula for local agency employees; also applicable to state employees hired January 15, 2011 through December 31, 2012 (State Misc Tier 2).

formula.structure: Age Factor × Years of Service Credit × Final Compensation = Annual Pension

ageFactor.note: Age factor increases gradually from 50 to 63. Same maximum factor as 2%@55 formula (2.418% at 63).

finalCompensation.description: Average annual compensation over highest 36 consecutive months

typical_local.note: Varies by employer contract. State Misc Tier 2 (hired 1/15/2011-12/31/2012): contribution rates set by collective bargaining, typically 7-8%.

pepra_misc_2pct_at_62.prevalence: Universal PEPRA miscellaneous formula — applies to all new CalPERS miscellaneous members hired on or after January 1, 2013. As of 2026, ~68% of active CalPERS members are PEPRA members.

formula.structure: Age Factor × Years of Service Credit × Final Compensation = Annual Pension

ageFactor.note: Full benefit (2.0%) at 62. Members may retire at 52 with 5 years of service. Age factor reaches maximum 2.5% at age 67. Factor increases in quarter-year increments.

finalCompensation.description: Average annual pensionable compensation over highest 36 consecutive months. PEPRA always uses 3-year average — no 1-year option.
finalCompensation.note: Key distinction from Classic 2%@55 (state members): PEPRA requires 36-month average vs 12-month highest year for state members.

compensationCap.note: Salary above cap not used in final compensation calculation. Caps adjust annually.

earlyRetirement.note: Reduced benefit from age 52. No retirement before 52 under PEPRA.

combinedClassicPepra.note: Members with combined Classic and PEPRA service may retire at age 50 for Classic portion. Minimum PEPRA retirement age remains 52 for PEPRA service.

memberContribution.mechanism: 50% of total normal cost, set annually by CalPERS actuarial valuation

fy2025_26_school.note: 8.00% for PEPRA school members in FY 2025-26 — unchanged because total normal cost did not change by more than 1%.

public_agency_varies.note: PEPRA public agency member contribution rates vary annually by plan actuarial valuation. Members should consult their agency's Rate Summary in myCalPERS.

memberContribution.rate_change_trigger: If total normal cost changes by more than 1% of payroll from base rate, member contribution must be adjusted to 50% of new total normal cost, rounded to nearest 0.25%.

classic_safety_3pct_at_50.prevalence: Most generous Classic safety formula — historically available to local safety (police/fire) hired before January 1, 2013 at agencies contracting for this benefit. Many large California cities and counties use 3%@50.

formula.structure: Age Factor × Years of Service Credit × Final Compensation = Annual Pension

maximum.note: 3% is both the minimum and maximum age factor — flat rate regardless of age beyond 50. No benefit to delaying retirement beyond normal eligibility under this formula.

ageFactor.note: Flat 3% age factor at any eligible retirement age. There is no increase for retiring later.

finalCompensation.note: Typically highest 1-year for state safety; local agencies may contract for 1-year or 3-year final compensation depending on their CalPERS contract.

exampleBenefit.scenario: Safety officer, age 50, 25 years service, $100,000 final compensation
exampleBenefit.note: 25-year cap effectively reached at 83.33% (beyond 27.78 years at 3% → 83.3% of final comp). No statutory cap on benefit percentage under 3%@50.

typical_local.note: Classic local safety 3%@50 member contributions vary by employer contract. Common rates in the 9-12% range. Employer-paid member contributions (EPMC) have historically been negotiated in some agency contracts.

pepra_safety_2_7pct_at_57.prevalence: Most common PEPRA safety formula under Government Code 7522.25(d). Applies to the majority of new local safety members (police, fire) hired on or after January 1, 2013.

formula.structure: Age Factor × Years of Service Credit × Final Compensation = Annual Pension

ageFactor.note: Factor reaches maximum 2.7% at age 57. No increase beyond 57.

finalCompensation.description: Average annual pensionable compensation over highest 36 consecutive months — always 3 years for PEPRA.

compensationCap.note: PEPRA pensionable compensation cap applies. See compensationLimits section for annual figures.

memberContribution.mechanism: 50% of total normal cost, determined annually

typical_rate.note: Typical PEPRA safety member contribution approximately 12-14% of pensionable compensation depending on plan and actuarial year. Example from City of Tustin FY2025-26: total normal cost 27.74%, employee rate = 13.87%.

ab_1383.description: AB 1383 (2025-26) would establish new safety formulas effective January 1, 2027: 2.5%@55, 2.7%@55, and 3%@55 — replacing current PEPRA safety formulas at 2%@57, 2.5%@57, and 2.7%@57. Bill is in the California legislature as of March 2026.

additionalFormulas._note: Less common formulas summarized here for reference. Not fully modeled.

classic_safety_2pct_at_50.note: Second-tier Classic local safety formula. Age factor: 2.0% at 50, increasing to maximum 2.7% at 55. Final comp: typically 3 years. Available at local agencies contracting for this tier.

classic_state_safety_a_2_5pct_at_55.note: Applies to state employees in safety classifications first employed prior to January 15, 2011. Normal retirement age 55. Contributions: 10% + 1% (state safety supplement).

classic_state_safety_b_2pct_at_55.note: Applies to state safety employees first hired January 15, 2011 through December 31, 2012.

pepra_safety_2_5pct_at_57.note: Intermediate PEPRA safety formula under Gov. Code 7522.25(c). Less common than 2.7%@57. Normal age 57, max factor 2.5%.

pepra_safety_2pct_at_57.note: Lowest PEPRA safety formula under Gov. Code 7522.25(b). Normal age 57, max factor 2.0%. Least common PEPRA safety formula.

fundingStatus.note: FY2024-25 preliminary net investment return of 11.6% increased funded status to ~79%. Employer contributions remain at elevated levels due to unfunded accrued liability from prior years.

employerContributions.note: Employer contribution rates vary by agency and plan tier. State and school employer rates are set by CalPERS Board annually. Public agency rates are set through individual agency actuarial valuations.

schools_fy2025_26.note: Schools employer pool rate for FY 2025-26: 26.81% of payroll. Decrease of 0.24% from FY 2024-25.

public_agency_normal_cost_examples.note: Example from City of Tustin FY2025-26 actuarial summary (representative): Classic Misc 2%@55 employer normal cost 27.38%; Classic Misc 2%@60 20.15%; PEPRA Misc 2%@62 13.99%; Classic Safety 3%@50 — not shown; PEPRA Safety 2.7%@57 ~27.74% total normal cost.

healthBenefits.description: CalPERS also administers health benefits for active and retired members — the largest purchaser of public employee health services in the US outside the federal government. Health benefits are separate from the pension defined benefit plan.

minimumService_csu.note: CSU retirees: 5 years = partial subsidy, 10 years = full state contribution

retireeEligibility.note: Retirees must retire within 120 days of separation to qualify for health benefits in retirement.

reciprocity.note: Reciprocity allows members who move between participating systems to retire concurrently and preserve benefit rights. Must file for reciprocity within six months of leaving one system and joining another.

industrialDisability.description: Industrial disability retirement available for members whose disability is job-related. No minimum age requirement. Benefit: greater of 50% of final compensation (miscellaneous) or 75% of final compensation (safety) or formula amount.
industrialDisability.note: CalPERS disability benefits are not modeled in detail here.

sources.name: CalPERS FY2025-26 State Employer/Employee Contribution Rates Circular Letter 200-025-25
CalPERS FY2025-26 Schools Contribution Rates Circular Letter 200-027-25
CalPERS FY2024-25 Preliminary Investment Return Announcement
CalPERS New Pension Contracts — Formula and Vesting Overview
PEPRA Blog Post — CalPERS PERSpective Feb 2026
